title: Habit-Loop Engineering

content.statement: Repeated behavior becomes automatic when properly structured.
content.question: What's your business's trigger, action, reward loop? Is it automatic, or does the user have to remember you?
content.equation: HLE = Trigger × Action Simplicity × Reward × Frequency
sop: Identify the Trigger that could bring a customer back, a notification, a habit, a recurring need.
Rate Action Simplicity 1-10, how easy the next step is once triggered (see calculation, page 6).
Rate the Reward 1-10, how satisfying the outcome feels immediately after the action (see calculation, page 6).
Rate Frequency 1-10, how often this loop could realistically repeat (see calculation, page 6).
Multiply all four scores together (see calculation, page 6), and if the score is low, fix the weakest link, usually Trigger or Reward, before adding more features.
content.explainer: Products without loops require marketing forever. Products with loops retain without effort.
questions: What's your business's trigger, action, reward loop? Is it automatic, or does the user have to remember you?
What reminds a customer to come back to you, an external notification, or their own memory, which fades?
Does using your product actually feel rewarding in the moment, or just useful in a way people forget to appreciate?
content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. Trigger 7, Action Simplicity 8, Reward 6, Frequency 5. 7 times 8 times 6 times 5 equals 1,680. Higher means the loop runs on its own, without you having to remind people. Lower means every return visit requires active marketing to make happen.
content.closing_statement: Habits retain users long after marketing stops working.

caption:
Principle Nº 82: Habit-Loop Engineering

Repeated behavior becomes automatic when properly structured. Products without loops require marketing forever.

Ask yourself: What's your business's trigger, action, reward loop? Is it automatic, or does the user have to remember you?
